title: 10 best herbal tinctures for nausea

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with nausea because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which work to calm the digestive system and alleviate symptoms associated with motion sickness, morning sickness, and chemotherapy. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, bind to receptors in the stomach, reducing inflammation and soothing the digestive tract. This results in a reduction of nausea and vomiting, allowing for a smoother and more comfortable digestive process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects that help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating nausea and discomfort.

Digestive enzymes: Ginger stimulates the production of digestive enzymes in the stomach, such as gastric amylase and lipase, which helps to break down food more efficiently and absorb nutrients properly, reducing nausea and bloating.

Antioxidants and free radical scavengers: Ginger's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the digestive system, contributing to nausea and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, specifically 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce nausea and vomiting by inhibiting the release of nausea-inducing neurotransmitters.

Shogaols: Shogaols, particularly 6-shogaol, have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to alleviate nausea and vomiting by reducing pain perception and inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract.

Zingiberene: Zingiberene is a sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties, which help to reduce nausea and vomiting by inhibiting the release of nausea-inducing neurotransmitters and reducing inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used to make tinctures for nausea due to their ability to ease digestion and reduce inflammation in the stomach.

Buds: The buds of Zingiber officinale are occasionally used to make tinctures for nausea due to their potential to stimulate digestion and reduce nausea, although they are less commonly used than the rhizomes or roots.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of fresh zingiber officinale roots and 500 milliliters of 25 percent ethanol as ingredients.
Chop the zingiber officinale roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped zingiber officinale roots with the ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 4 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with nausea because of its natural ability to relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing inflammation and calming digestive issues. The menthol content in peppermint oil also has a soothing effect on the digestive tract, helping to reduce spasms and cramping that can contribute to nausea. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of peppermint may help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), which is often linked with nausea. By using herbal peppermint tinctures, individuals can naturally alleviate nausea and promote a sense of calm and digestive comfort.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Peppermint oil in tincture form can stimulate digestion and improve gut motility, helping to relieve nausea caused by slow or blocked digestive systems.
Anti-Inflammatory: The menthol content in peppermint tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining that may be contributing to nausea symptoms.

Gastroprotective Agent: Peppermint tincture's antacid and anti-inflammatory properties also offer gastroprotective effects, providing relief from nausea caused by acid reflux, stomach ulcers, or other digestive disorders.
remedy_constituents: Menthone: Menthone is a terpene that has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
Menthol: Menthol, another terpene, has been shown to have a calming effect on the stomach, helping to reduce nausea and vomiting by relaxing the stomach muscles and improving digestion.
Linalool: Linalool is a phenolic terpene that has been found to have a sedative effect, which can help alleviate nausea by reducing stress and anxiety, two common causes of nausea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used to make tinctures for nausea due to their high concentration of menthol, which helps to calm the stomach.
Stems: Stems are used for tinctures because they contain menthol and other compounds that can help alleviate nausea and digestive issues.
Roots: Roots are used in tinctures for their ability to regulate digestive processes and help reduce nausea due to their bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated for best quality.
Dry the harvested mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 1-2 weeks.
Combine 1 part dried mentha x piperita with 2 parts 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and seal it.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar daily to aid extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass tinctures helps with nausea because of its natural ability to calm the stomach and alleviate digestive discomfort. The herb's soothing properties help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting. Additionally, lemongrass contains compounds that have a gentle sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to ease anxiety and stress-related nausea. By promoting relaxation and reducing digestive upset, lemongrass tinctures can provide fast and effective relief from nausea symptoms.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Lemongrass tincture helps to dispel gas and reduce bloating, which can alleviate nausea symptoms by allowing for easier digestion and reducing pressure on the stomach.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate nausea caused by gastrointestinal issues such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic properties of lemongrass tincture can help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and spasms that can contribute to nausea.
remedy_constituents: Geranial: Helps alleviate nausea due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote digestive relaxation.
Citronellal: Exhibits anti-emetic properties, helping to calm the stomach and reduce nausea by blocking the vomiting center in the brain.
Limonene: Demonstrates anti-inflammatory and digestive soothing effects, which can help alleviate nausea and promote a sense of well-being.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in citral and other essential oils, which are primarily responsible for the anti-nausea and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems of Cymbopogon citratus also contain essential oils, including citral, which helps in alleviating nausea and digestive issues.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of lemongrass are used to make tinctures due to their high content of essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory and anti-nausea properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 pounds of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and stems when they are in full bloom for optimal potency.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces and dry them in a single layer for 7 days.
Combine 10 pounds of dried plant material with 5 liters of 95% ethanol in a large container.
Steep the plant material in the ethanol solution for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional stirring.
Strain the liquid mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids then bottle the tincture in dark glass containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Papaya tinctures helps with nausea because it contains papain, an enzyme that breaks down proteins and reduces inflammation in the digestive tract. This anti-inflammatory property helps to soothe the stomach and reduce symptoms of nausea. Additionally, papaya's natural antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the digestive system, allowing for smoother digestion and reducing feelings of queasiness. By calming the stomach and promoting a healthy gut environment, papaya tinctures provide natural relief from nausea and indigestion.
remedy_properties: Papain: Papain, an enzyme found in papaya, helps to break down proteins and reduce inflammation in the stomach, which can alleviate nausea and vomiting.
Vitamin C: Vitamin C in papaya tincture has antioxidant properties, which can help to neutralize free radicals that may contribute to nausea and other gastrointestinal issues.
Fiber: The fiber content of papaya tincture can help to regulate bowel movements, reduce inflammation, and soothe the digestive tract, all of which can help alleviate nausea and related symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Vitamins: Vitamins, particularly vitamins A and C, present in Carica papaya tinctures, help alleviate nausea by providing antioxidant properties and supporting the overall health of the digestive system.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, found in Carica papaya, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting by soothing the digestive tract.

Papain: Papain, a proteolytic enzyme in Carica papaya, aids in digestion and helps break down proteins, which can contribute to nausea and discomfort, thereby providing relief from nausea and promoting a healthy digestive system.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The most commonly used part for making tinctures due to their high papain content, which helps alleviate nausea.
Leaves: Used to make tinctures due to their anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe digestive issues, including nausea.
Seeds: Utilized for making tinctures due to their high concentration of carotenoids and other compounds that may help alleviate nausea and digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh carica papaya fruit at its optimal ripeness.
Chop 2 pounds of fresh carica papaya fruit into small pieces in a glass bowl.
Combine the chopped fruit with 2 cups of 190 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with nausea because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds. The tannins, flavonoids, and alkaloids present in the tree's leaves have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe the digestive system and reduce symptoms of nausea. Additionally, the Maidenhair tree's natural ability to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation may also contribute to its effectiveness in alleviating nausea and promoting a sense of well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress, inflammation, and damage to stomach lining caused by nausea-inducing substances, alleviating symptoms.

Vasodilatory effects: Ginkgo's ability to dilate blood vessels can help improve blood flow to the stomach, reducing nausea and vomiting associated with digestive issues like gastritis or motion sickness.
GABA-like activity: Ginkgo's GABA-mimetic properties can help regulate nervous system activity, calming anxiety and stress-induced nausea by promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of queasiness.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate nausea and vomiting by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract.

Bilobalide: Bilobalide, a sesquiterpene lactone, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that may contribute to its anti-nausea properties by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach and intestines.

Ginkgolides: Ginkgolides, a group of sesquiterpene lactones, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that may help alleviate nausea and vomiting by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the gastrointestinal tract, and also by modulating neurotransmitters and improving blood flow to the stomach and intestines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are the most commonly used part for tinctures due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which provide the medicinal properties.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used to make tinctures due to their ability to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, helping alleviate nausea.
Barks: Ginkgo biloba barks are used in tinctures to help reduce nausea due to their reported anti-inflammatory properties and ability to improve digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 pounds of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop the ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces to release their active compounds and essential oils.
Combine 2 cups of the chopped leaves with 4 cups of vodka in a clean glass container at a 1:2 leaf-to-solvent ratio.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the container every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with nausea because of its unique ability to calm the digestive system and ease stomach upset. The calming properties of lavender work to soothe the muscles in the stomach, reducing inflammation and spasms that can contribute to feelings of queasiness. Additionally, lavender's natural sedative effects help to quiet the mind and reduce stress, further alleviating nausea symptoms. remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: English Lavender tincture helps to relax the digestive muscles, reducing spasms and cramping associated with nausea and vomiting.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) property: The calming effects of lavender can help alleviate anxiety-related nausea, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing stress levels that contribute to nausea.
Anti-inflammatory property: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting by irritating the stomach lining.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps with nausea by exerting a sedative effect on the stomach and promoting relaxation, which can reduce nausea and vomiting.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene also helps with nausea by exhibiting a calming effect on the nervous system and reducing inflammation in the stomach, which can alleviate nausea and vomiting.
Luteolin: This flavonoid helps with nausea by inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators and reducing inflammation in the stomach, which can alleviate nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate nausea.
Flowers: They are used due to their calming and sedative effects, which can help reduce nausea and promote relaxation.
Stems: They are used due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate nausea and promote digestive health.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and 2 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried flowers with 80 proof vodka in a 1:2 ratio and seal the jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks to allow for extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container to remove the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass dropper bottles and store in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with nausea because of its natural ability to soothe the stomach and calm the digestive system. The apigenin and luteolin in chamomile work to reduce inflammation and relax muscle contractions, which can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting. Additionally, the anti-anxiety effects of chamomile tincture can help to reduce stress and anxiety-induced nausea. By promoting relaxation and calming the stomach, chamomile tinctures provide a natural remedy for alleviating nausea and restoring digestive balance.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Chamomile's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects can help alleviate nausea by calming the nervous system and reducing feelings of unease.
Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile's antispasmodic effects can help soothe stomach muscles, reduce spasms, and ease nausea caused by digestive issues such as IBS or Crohn's disease.

Gastroprotective properties: Chamomile has been shown to possess gastroprotective effects, which means it can help shield the stomach lining from irritation and inflammation, reducing nausea and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as an anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic agent, helping to relax the stomach muscles and reduce nausea.
Alpha-bisabolol: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe the digestive tract and alleviate nausea.
Chamazulene: This sesquiterpene is known for its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make tinctures for nausea because of their high content of apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory and calming properties.
Leaves: They are used to make tinctures for nausea because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help to soothe the digestive system.
Seeds: They are used to make tinctures for nausea because they contain apigenin, luteolin, and other compounds that may help to alleviate nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of the dried flowers of matricaria chamomilla, and 1 part of a suitable solvent like vodka.
Combine the dried flowers with the solvent in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 5 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with nausea because of its natural ability to calm the stomach and ease digestive discomfort. The active compounds in fennel, such as anethole and limonene, have been shown to reduce inflammation and spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating symptoms of nausea and vomiting. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to expel gas and bloating, further contributing to a sense of relief from nausea. As a result, herbal fennel tinctures can provide natural and effective relief for individuals experiencing nausea.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel tincture's carminative properties help to release trapped gas and reduce bloating, which can contribute to feelings of nausea.
Antispasmodic properties: Fennel tincture's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can cause nausea.
Gastroprotective properties: Fennel tincture's gastroprotective properties help to protect the stomach lining from irritation, reducing inflammation and damage that can lead to nausea.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: This monoterpene acts as an antispasmodic, helping to relax the muscles in the stomach and alleviate nausea by reducing spasms.
Anethole: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe the stomach, reduce inflammation, and calm nausea.
Foeniculin: This sesquiterpene has been shown to possess antiemetic properties, helping to prevent and alleviate nausea by interacting with neurotransmitters that regulate the vomiting reflex.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Foeniculum vulgare seeds are commonly used due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly anethole, which is responsible for its anti-nausea properties.
Leaves: Foeniculum vulgare leaves are often used for tinctures due to their moderate essential oil content, which helps to soothe digestive issues and alleviate nausea.

Fruits (Fennel Bulb): The fruit of Foeniculum vulgare, also known as the fennel bulb, is used for its mild flavor and essential oil content, which makes it effective in treating digestive issues and nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried foeniculum vulgare herb in a ratio of 1:2 with solvent.
Combine the dried herb with 80 proof vodka in a glass jar to a 1:2 ratio.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a glass container.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and label with the date and ingredients used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with nausea because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the digestive tract. The root of the licorice plant contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce irritation and discomfort in the stomach and intestines. Additionally, licorice tincture's sweet flavor can be soothing to the taste buds and provide a sense of relief from nausea symptoms. As a natural remedy, herbal licorice tinctures offer a gentle yet effective way to alleviate nausea without relying on harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach and intestines, which can contribute to nausea.

Flavonoids: Licorice tinctures contain flavonoids such as glabridin and isoliquiritigenin, which have been shown to possess antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing nausea and vomiting.

Phlegm- dispelling properties: Licorice root has a long history of use in traditional Chinese medicine for its ability to dispel phlegm (mucus) from the body, which can become trapped in the digestive system and cause nausea, bloating, and other uncomfortable symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps reduce nausea by blocking the action of stomach acid, thereby reducing irritation and inflammation in the digestive tract.
Liquiritin: Has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation and soothing the stomach lining.
Licoric acid: Acts as an anti-spasmodic agent, helping to relax the stomach muscles and reduce nausea caused by digestive spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties, which help alleviate nausea.
Roots: Contain flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help soothe nausea and digestive issues.
Roots: Rich in ascorbic acid, which acts as a natural antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent that can help reduce nausea and promote digestive health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the autumn when the plant is mature for optimal quality.
Clean the roots thoroughly with water to remove dirt and debris from the harvesting process.
Chop 1 part of the cleaned roots into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Steep the chopped roots in 5 parts of a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin at a ratio of 1:5 for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the glycosides from the solvent.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai tinctures helps with nausea because they contain a unique blend of bioactive compounds that target the underlying causes of queasiness. The tincture's gentle stimulation of the digestive system helps to ease stomach upset and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms of nausea and vomiting. Additionally, dong quai's natural adaptogenic properties help to balance the body's hormonal fluctuations, which can contribute to nausea in women, making it an effective remedy for menstrual-related discomfort and morning sickness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties: Dong Quai's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid, help reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can alleviate nausea caused by digestive issues.
Vasodilatory effects: The tincture's ability to dilate blood vessels helps improve blood flow to the digestive system, reducing cramping and discomfort that can exacerbate nausea.

Antioxidant and anti-oxidative stress properties: Dong Quai's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to nausea and digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic glycosides (specifically, senkyunolide and aciphyllone): These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
Angelica lactone: This compound has been found to have a sedative effect on the gastrointestinal tract, which can help calm nausea and vomiting by reducing the activity of the stomach muscles.

Coumarins (specifically, coumarin and scopoletin): These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract, contributing to the relief of nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: These are the most commonly used part of Angelica sinensis due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used because they contain a higher amount of essential oils and flavonoids compared to other parts.
Leaves: Leaves are used to make tinctures for nausea due to their rich content of phenolic acids and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Gather clean and dry angelica sinensis roots weighing about 250 grams for the tincture recipe.
Combine the angelica sinensis roots with 75 percent 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Store the herbal tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years after the preparation is complete.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with nausea because it contains a unique blend of alkaloids and flavonoids that work together to calm the digestive system. The herb has been traditionally used to ease stomach upset, bloating, and cramping, which are often associated with nausea. Additionally, maypop's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce irritation in the gut, allowing for smoother digestion and reducing the likelihood of nausea. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried passionflower flowers at dawn on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Chop 250 grams of the harvested passionflower flowers into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped passionflower flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for eight weeks, shaking daily, to allow extraction and maceration.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids, and label with date preparation time and ingredients used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with nausea because of its natural sedative properties, which help to calm the stomach and reduce feelings of queasiness. The herbal remedy has been used for centuries to treat a range of digestive issues, including nausea, vomiting, and irritable bowel syndrome. By soothing the digestive tract and promoting relaxation, valerian tinctures can help to alleviate symptoms of nausea and provide quick relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anxiolytic effects: Valerian root has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress-related nausea.
Antispasmodic properties: Valerian's antispasmodic compounds can help relax the muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to nausea.

Gastroprotective effects: Valerian has been shown to have a protective effect on the mucous membranes in the stomach and intestines, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation that may exacerbate nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots have a strong sedative effect, which helps to alleviate nausea and vomiting by calming the stomach and gut.
Rhizomes: The rhizome of the valerian plant is rich in valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for its medicinal properties, including relief from nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Clean and chop 100g of the harvested valeriana roots into small pieces.
Combine the chopped roots with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar leaving one inch at the top.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6-8 weeks shaking the jar daily to promote extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle and store.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Catnip tinctures helps with nausea because of its unique composition of essential oils, specifically nepetalactone. This natural compound has been shown to interact with receptors in the brain that regulate vomiting and nausea. When ingested, nepetalactone binds to these receptors, calming the stomach and reducing feelings of queasiness. Additionally, catnip's sedative properties can also help relax the digestive system, further alleviating symptoms of nausea and promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (calming) effects: Catnip's anxiolytic properties can help reduce feelings of anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for nausea, allowing the body to focus on recovery rather than exacerbating symptoms.

Gastrointestinal soothing: Catnip's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic compounds can help soothe and relax the digestive tract, reducing inflammation, cramping, and discomfort associated with nausea and vomiting.

Vomiting inhibition: Catnip's essential oils have been shown to inhibit vomiting in animal studies, which suggests that it may be effective in reducing the frequency and intensity of nausea-induced vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the catnip plant contain a high concentration of essential oils and sesquiterpenes, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate nausea.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of catnip, also known as underground stems, are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather one part dried nepeta cataria herb in flower and leaf form for every three parts of high-proof vodka or ethanol at least 90%.
Combine the dry herb with the vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and store it in a cool dark place.
Allow the mixture to steep for four to six weeks shaking the jar daily to ensure all particles are saturated by the solvent.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and storing the liquid in the refrigerator.
After two months transfer the tincture to dark glass dropper bottles with tight-fitting lids and label them clearly indicating the ingredients dosages preparations times and expiration date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops tinctures helps with nausea because they contain flavonoids and lupulin, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract. remedy_properties: Xanthohumol: Hops contain a flavonoid called xanthohumol, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms of nausea.

Lupulone: Lupulone is a bitter compound found in hops that has been shown to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and nausea by increasing the contraction of the stomach muscles and promoting the elimination of toxins from the digestive tract.

Humulus lupulus acids: The acids present in hops, particularly humulone and lupulone, have been shown to have anti-emetic properties, helping to reduce the frequency and severity of nausea and vomiting episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the hops plant have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea, due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Hops buds are rich in essential oils that help to soothe the stomach and relieve symptoms of nausea, making them a popular choice for natural remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh humulus lupulus leaves and flowers or use dried forms of the plant.
Combine one part humulus lupulus with one part vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another container, discarding solids after 20 minutes.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store at room temperature for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with nausea because it contains rosmarinic acid, a natural compound that has been shown to have potent anti-nausea properties. This acid works by binding to receptors in the stomach and gut, helping to reduce inflammation and spasms that can cause queasiness. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers for drying.
Dry the herbs in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at low heat for several hours.
Grind 1 part dried melissa officinalis into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle then mix with 5 parts high-proof vodka or neutral spirit.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking daily then strain through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place and take 10-20 drops in water or under the tongue as needed to alleviate nausea.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves from trusted sources in morning when dew is still present on the plants.
Clean and chop 20-30 grams of harvested salvia leaves into small pieces using scissors or sharp knife.
Combine chopped leaves with 50% vodka or high-proof grain alcohol and allow mixture to steep for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain tincture through cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Store tincture in cool dark place for at least 2 months before using, taking 20-30 drops three times daily to alleviate nausea symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried artemisia dracunculus leaves and stems according to your preferred quantity and quality standards.
Chop 200g of harvested material into smaller pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine chopped material with 400ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring all plant matter is fully submerged.
Store the mixture at room temperature for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate solute transfer and maceration.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding solids, and label the tincture with date prepared and ingredient ratio.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for nausea are concentrated plant extracts that help alleviate nausea and its associated symptoms. They work by targeting the underlying causes of nausea, such as digestive issues or stress. Effective herbal tinctures for nausea include Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Piper methysticum, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cinchona officinalis, Avena sativa, Foeniculum vulgare, Passiflora incarnata, Lavandula angustifolia, and Angelica archangelica. If left untreated, nausea can lead to dehydration, weight loss, and strained relationships due to decreased social participation and fatigue.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nausea that are treated with herbal tinctures are digestive issues such as diarrhea, bloating, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbal remedies like peppermint, ginger, and dandelion root have anti-inflammatory and carminative properties that can help soothe the stomach and reduce symptoms. Additionally, tinctures made from herbs like chamomile, lemon balm, and passionflower can also alleviate anxiety and stress-related discomforts, which often manifest as nausea-like symptoms.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with nausea is typically a blend of ginger, peppermint, and licorice root. Ginger tincture has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the stomach, while peppermint tincture calms the digestive system and reduces discomfort. Licorice root tincture helps to balance stomach acid and alleviate nausea. This combination can be taken in small doses, 2-3 times a day, and can be mixed with water or taken sublingually for optimal absorption and relief from nausea.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Obstetrics and gynecology, ginger tinctures can be effective in alleviating nausea due to their active compound, gingerol, which was found to significantly decrease visual analog scores of posttherapy minus baseline nausea by 2.1 +/- 1.9 in women with nausea and vomiting of pregnancy. This reduction in nausea severity was also accompanied by a decrease in the number of vomiting episodes from 1.4 +/- 1.3 in the ginger group compared to 0.3 +/- 1.1 in the placebo group. The results suggest that ginger tinctures, containing similar active compounds, may be beneficial in treating nausea in pregnant women by reducing symptoms and improving overall well-being.

By reducing inflammation and improving digestion, ginger tinctures provide quick relief from nausea, making them a popular natural remedy for individuals experiencing stomach upset. This leads to a reduction in symptoms.
